figures.caption: Figure 6: Numerical performance of the PR-TENG framework on the Acoustic Wave Equation (a) Comparison of the Hamiltonian error for utarget with and without the relaxation scheme, illustrating the mitigation of energy drift. (b) Comparative performance of the PR-TENG and the original TENG algorithms.
Figure 7: Relative error of RP-TENG on the acoustic wave equation

Figure 1: Evolution of the mass conservation error over time. The projection method (blue solid line) maintains the error near machine precision (∼ 10−14), significantly outperforming the original method (orange dashed line) which exhibits fluctuations between 10−9 and 10−5.

Figure 5: Comprehensive performance on the KdV equation soliton simulation. (a) Temporal evolution of the relative L2 error against the analytical solution.(b) Long-term energy-preserving capability of the RP-TENG framework for t ∈ [0, 10]

Figure 2: Numerical performance of the TENG framework on the inviscid Burgers’ equation. (a) Mass conservation error relative to different time step sizes and the projection method. (b) Temporal evolution of the relative L2 error, highlighting the accuracy gains from the projection-based physical constraint.
Figure 8: RP-TENG long time implement effects

Figure 4: Numerical performance of RP-TENG on the KdV equation. (a) Energy conservation: RP-TENG maintains the Hamiltonian error |H(u0) − H(ûθn )| near machine epsilon, eliminating the drift of vanilla TENG. (b) Parameter stability: The temporal consistency of γ ≈ 1 confirms the non-intrusiveness of the projection step within the dual-constraint framework.

Figure 3: Numerical performance of the TENG framework on the KdV equation. (a) Necessity of the relaxation method: The RP-TENG framework suppresses the quadratic invariant error |H(utarget) − H(ûθn )| to machine epsilon (∼ 10−15), effectively eliminating the numerical drift of the baseline. (b) Influence on dynamical trajectory: The stability of γ ≈ 1 confirms that the energy constraint is enforced with minimal intrusiveness to the neural dynamical trajectory.
